channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 The Gulf Crisis Is Forcing Japan Into an Energy Rethink | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tokyo spent decades preparing for disruption—then found its options narrowing.

item.title: 🔐 AI makes it easy to expose holes in Army’s unified network, official says | Breaking Defense
item.description: New AI capabilities are “lowering the barrier of entry” to exploit vulnerabilities, according to the Army’s chief data officer.

item.title: 🌏 How will the Iran war change the US role in the world? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts weigh in on whether the United States should “pivot” its attention away from the Middle East and toward the Indo-Pacific. The post How will the Iran war change the US role in the world? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🔐 How defensive cyber responds to hockey-stick growth of AI-driven threats | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Artificial intelligence is reshaping adversarial cyber operations at machine speed, requiring defenders to increase operational tempo while imposing pain and cost.

item.title: 📍 New bill aims to regulate military uses of AI | Defense One
item.description: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand also wants to establish a clear chain of human accountability for AI on the battlefield.

item.title: 📍 Five charts that show Latin America’s search for economic growth starts in health systems | Atlantic Council
item.description: Health investments could boost economic productivity significantly—if the countries direct their expenditures toward efforts that tangibly improve public health. The post Five charts that show Latin America’s search for economic growth starts in health systems appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 Does the Quad Still Matter? | War on the Rocks
item.description: On May 26, India hosted a formal meeting of the foreign ministers of the Quad — comprising the United States, Australia, India, and Japan. Since its initial creation in 2007 and revival in 2017, foreign policy analysts have debated the usefulness of the organization, which was…

item.title: 🏴 Rheinmetall locks in wide-ranging $6.6 billion weapons package with Romania | Breaking Defense
item.description: Romania is due to become the second Lynx operator on NATO’s eastern flank after Hungary’s 2020 order of 218 vehicles.

item.title: 📍 Future presence of US troops in Lithuania is ‘under review,’ says minister | Defense News
item.description: U.S. troops presently stationed in the country are now leaving as expected, but the next scheduled group is not arriving, the defense minister said.

item.title: 📍 Are Ukrainian Mirage 2000s Now Flying Air-To-Ground Strike Missions? | The War Zone
item.description: A low-level run followed by a steep climb hints at a ground-attack mission as part of the Mirage’s expanding combat repertoire. The post Are Ukrainian Mirage 2000s Now Flying Air-To-Ground Strike Missions? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Trump signs business-friendlier version of AI executive order | Defense One
item.description: The order asks for just a month of pre-publication access to advanced models, not the 90 days of an earlier draft.

item.title: 🏛️ Next Generation Penetrator Bomb Slated To Replace MOP Has Been Designated GBU-76 | The War Zone
item.description: The official designation comes as the Air Force plans to field the GBU-76 take shape. The post Next Generation Penetrator Bomb Slated To Replace MOP Has Been Designated GBU-76 appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Braw in Economist Enterprise on the benefits of decreased globalization | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 2, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw mentioned in an article in the Economist Enterprise on how the end of globalization could bring unexpected benefits. The post Braw in Economist Enterprise on the benefits of decreased globalization…

item.title: 🏴 Braw in The Guardian on the importance of citizen resilience | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 2, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw published an article in The Guardian discussing the importance of civilian preparedness and how NATO allies can find inspiration in the Scandinavian and Baltic regions. The post Braw in The Guardian on the…

item.title: 🔐 DoD CIO implores industry to put a greater focus on ‘foundational cybersecurity’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Compliance does not equal security. It did not when I was in industry, and it does not from my seat where I am today,” DoD CIO Kirsten Davie said.

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s defense exports reach record $19 billion in 2025 | Breaking Defense
item.description: “[M]issile, rocket, and air defense systems continued to lead in 2025, accounting for over a quarter (29%) of total deal volume,” the Israeli Ministry of Defense announced today.

item.title: 📍 Celebrating 40 years of Defense News | Defense News
item.description: Since 1986, Defense News has been the authoritative voice for the world's defense decision-makers.

item.title: 🌍 France restricts Israeli presence at Europe’s biggest defense show | Defense News
item.description: The restrictions come amid strained relations between Paris and Jerusalem over Israel’s military operations in Lebanon.

item.title: 🌍 How long will it take to replenish the US military’s munitions stockpiles? | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, we look at how long it might take to restock thousands of US munitions used against Iran.

item.title: 🏴 Kazakhstan, Russia Step Forward With $16.4 Billion Nuclear Power Plant Plan | The Diplomat
item.description: Moscow is reportedly furnishing as much as 85 percent of the financing for the project.

item.title: 🏴 US, NATO allies to launch scaled-back Baltic Sea drills | Defense News
item.description: Officials say the smaller footprint reflects operational realities rather than waning commitment.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy selects companies for at-sea MUSV prototype testing | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Navy announced on Friday that it had selected seven companies to compete to secure the service’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel contract.

item.title: 🕵️ Trump appoints housing official to be acting director of national intelligence | Defense One
item.description: William Pulte, who lacks experience in intelligence, will retain his current post, where he has launched investigations into the president’s political foes.

item.title: 📍 Nepal’s Prime Minister Mistakes Mandate for Immunity | The Diplomat
item.description: Balendra Shah, who assumed office just two months ago, does as he pleases, with little regard for constitutional norms, parliamentary procedures or diplomatic niceties.

item.title: 🏛️ Here’s how a new Army directorate is delivering tech to soldiers in under 180 days | Breaking Defense
item.description: In an interview with Breaking Defense, Col. Christopher Hill, director of the Global Tactical Edge Acquisition Directorate, explained how the service’s shift in cultural norms is resulting in delivering tech at speed and at scale.

item.title: 🏴 Moscow’s New Military Partner Has Something Russia Needs More Than Allies | The Diplomat
item.description: The security provisions in recent agreements with the Taliban may reflect intent, but the more immediate benefit on the Russian side appears to be addressing the labor shortage.

item.title: 🌏 Beyond China and the US: How Japan is Incrementally Rebalancing Its Foreign Policy | The Diplomat
item.description: Neither the Japan-U.S. alliance nor the Sino-Japanese economic relationship will completely unravel overnight. However, the overall trendlines have led Tokyo to seek further hedges.

item.title: 🕵️ The ICESCR in Armed Conflict: Submission to the UN Committee on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights | Just Security
item.description: Experts argue ICESCR applies in armed conflict and caution against broad claims of international humanitarian law as lex specialis. The post The ICESCR in Armed Conflict: Submission to the UN Committee on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Unholy Violence: Weaponizing Religion Against Gender Rights Defenders in Myanmar | The Diplomat
item.description: Buddhist fundamentalism functions in post-coup Myanmar not as a parallel force to military authoritarianism, but as an integral component of it.

item.title: 🌏 In Addition to Chinese Pressure, a Backsliding Democracy May Explain Zambia’s Decision to Cancel a Major Human Rights Summit | Just Security
item.description: Zambia’s cancellation of RightsCon is an indication not only of China’s influence, but also the country's own democratic erosion under a government that promised otherwise. The post In Addition to Chinese Pressure, a Backsliding Democracy May Explain Zambia’s Decision to Cancel…
